steps.n: Added a short vowel (fatha) to make the calling word 'yaa' that gets someone's attention
Added a long alif to make the call stronger and longer
Added hamza with alif to make the call even more attention-grabbing
Added shadda to double the final letter, making it stronger
Added ها to make it a general address to a group
The word is already complete with all its parts - the vocative يا, the core أيّ, and the attention particle ها
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Doubling of middle letter shows intensity, command form shows it's an order
The مُ prefix shows who does the action. The first د combines with ث because they're similar sounds.

steps.m: O you who wraps himself! (addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: ٱلْ makes it specific, ُ shows it's being used as a title or address. This is connected to يٰٓأَيُّهَا which means 'O you!'
Added damma and sukun to make basic command form
Weak letter و is removed to make the command easier to say

steps.m: to warn or alert someone about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to a command form telling someone to warn (comes after قُمْ 'arise!' in the ayah)
Added sukun at the end to show it's a complete command form
Added فَ to show this command comes as a result of the previous command قُمْ (arise!)

steps.m: to raise, develop, bring to maturity

steps.n: The doubled ب shows this is someone who does a lot of nurturing/developing, making them a master or lord
Added كَ at the end to show this belongs to you (when speaking to one male). This word is being acted on by the command فَكَبِّرْ (magnify/glorify).
The وَ at the start means 'and', connecting this phrase to previous content

steps.m: to make great or declare greatness

steps.n: Added shadda to show intensive meaning of declaring or making something great
Changed from 'he magnified' to 'you magnify!' by changing middle vowel and adding stop mark at end to make it a command

steps.m: so magnify!/therefore declare greatness! (command to Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this command follows from what was mentioned before
Root meaning 'to clothe' becomes a simple noun meaning 'one piece of clothing'
Changes singular to broken plural form for garments
Adds second person singular possessive pronoun. This word is what is being purified in the command 'purify' (فَطَهِّرْ).
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect this with previous phrase
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The shadda doubles the middle letter to show doing something thoroughly
The ف at the start means 'so' or 'then' - it shows this action follows from something before it
Root letters got vowels (damma and sukun) to form a noun meaning filth
Added ال (the) at the beginning. The ل merges into the ر creating a shadda
Added و (and) at the beginning and fatha at the end because it's the object of the command 'abandon' (فَٱهْجُرْ) in the next ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to 'u' to make it a command to one person
Added helper alif to make the command easier to pronounce
Added فَ to show this command is a result of what came before
This is a negative particle that tells us not to do something. In this ayah, it's telling us not to do the action mentioned next (تَمْنُن meaning 'confer favors')
The و connects this command with what came before it, adding the meaning of 'and'. Together with لا it forms a connected negative command.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to make it a command (telling someone to do it)

steps.m: you bestow favors (connected to تَسْتَكْثِرُ - you seek increase)
steps.n: Added تَ at the beginning to show you are doing this action. This verb is connected to تَسْتَكْثِرُ (you seek increase) in the ayah.
This is the basic concept of something being a lot or many
Adding vowels turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'became many'
Added Form X pattern indicating seeking or requesting the meaning of the root

steps.m: you seek to increase (connected to تَمْنُن - you bestow favor)
steps.n: Changed to present tense and second person (you) to match the context of the verse where Allah is addressing the Prophet ﷺ. This verb is connected to تَمْنُن (you bestow favor) before it.
The doubled middle letter (shadda) shows this is someone who nurtures and cares for others perfectly and completely
Added كَ at the end to show this belongs to the person being spoken to (like saying 'your')
Added لِ at the start to show this is being done for or to someone (like saying 'to' or 'for')

steps.m: and for your lord (connected to فَٱصْبِرْ - so be patient)
steps.n: Added وَ at the start to connect this to the next word فَٱصْبِرْ (so be patient). The full meaning is 'and for your Lord, be patient.'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he was patient' to a command 'be patient!' by adding i- at the start and changing the vowels
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before
This is a time word that sets up what happens next. Here it introduces when the horn is blown

steps.m: and/then when (followed by نُقِرَ - is blown)
steps.n: The فَ prefix shows this is part of a sequence of events, connecting it to what happened before. It indicates 'then when' or 'and when' the horn is blown
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: it was blown (referring to الناقور - the trumpet)
steps.n: Changed from active (someone blowing) to passive (being blown) by changing the vowels. Connected to الناقور (the trumpet) that comes right after it.
Basic preposition showing location inside something. Here it shows the blowing will happen in/into the trumpet.
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Starting from the same root as نُقِرَ
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make past tense verb 'he tapped'
Adding ال makes it specific/definite
This is a demonstrative particle that points to something. It's made up of multiple parts that work together.
Adding ل with kasra (ِ ) changes it from pointing to something near to something far away
The ك at the end makes it specifically point back to something already mentioned

steps.m: so that (connecting to what came before)
steps.n: The فَ connects this word to what was said before, showing a result or sequence
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make the basic noun 'day'

Added ئِذٍ to show we're talking about a specific day that was mentioned before. The fatha (َ) before ئِذٍ shows this is happening 'on' or 'during' that day. This word connects to عَسِيرٌ (difficult) to tell us when something is difficult.
The root letters ي-و-م get vowels added to make the noun 'day'. The first letter gets a fat-ha (َ) and the middle letter gets a sukoon (ْ)
Adding two damma marks (ٌ) at the end makes it mean 'a day' instead of just 'day'
Added ي to show this is a strong quality - like saying something is 'really difficult'
Added tanween (two damma marks) to show this is one difficult thing among many
This is the basic form meaning something high or elevated

steps.m: upon, against (describing the difficulty for الْكٰفِرِينَ)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing something is on top of or against something else. In this ayah, it shows the difficulty is upon/against the disbelievers.

steps.m: to reject, deny, or be ungrateful
steps.n: The basic meaning relates to covering or concealing something
Added vowels to connect the letters into a past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he rejected) to a noun meaning 'one who rejects'
Added ال to make it specific
Added ين to show it's talking about many people
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun meaning 'other than'

steps.m: other than/different from (subject of يَسِيرٍ - easy)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject (doer) that يَسِيرٍ (easy) is describing. Together they mean 'not easy'.
Added ي between second and third root letters to make it a strong adjective
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this word is describing غَيْرُ (not) in the phrase 'not easy on the disbelievers'
The initial و is dropped in the command form. This is a special case with this verb.
Added sukun (ْ) on the ر to show where the word pauses

steps.m: leave me! (command to one person, addressing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added نِى at the end to show that 'me' is the object being left alone. This word is part of a longer statement where the speaker is asking Allah to handle someone.

steps.m: who/whoever (asking about the person mentioned in خَلَقْتُ)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about people. In this ayah, it's asking about who Allah created.

steps.m: and who/whoever (asking about the person mentioned in خَلَقْتُ)
steps.n: The و at the start connects this question to what came before (ذَرْنِى - leave me). So it's saying 'leave me, and [the one] who...'
Added fathas (َ) after each letter to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he created'

steps.m: I created (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end and a sukun (ْ) before it to show 'I' did the action. In this ayah, Allah is speaking about Himself.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the vowels and added ي to make it describe someone who is alone

steps.m: alone (describing the state of the one who was created in خَلَقْتُ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes the state of the one being created in the phrase 'whom I created'

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he' to 'I' by adding تُ at the end. The sukun (ْ) is added before تُ to help pronunciation.

steps.m: and I made (something into مَالًا مَّمْدُودًا/extended wealth)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This verb is connected to مَالًا مَّمْدُودًا (extended wealth) which shows what was made/given.
Added fatha to show how to pronounce the letter

steps.m: for him (connected to وَجَعَلْتُ - and I made)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who the action is for. This connects to the verb وَجَعَلْتُ (and I made) to show what was made for him.
Added a small و mark (ۥ) after هُ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
The middle letter و changes to ا. This word is what was given in the phrase 'I gave/made for him wealth.'
Added ًا at the end to show this is what's being given in the sentence 'I gave him wealth'
Changed into a word describing something that has been extended or stretched out

steps.m: greatly extended/stretched (something that was extended a lot)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the first م to show it's extended a lot

steps.m: greatly extended wealth (describing مَالًا/wealth from previous ayah)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is describing the wealth (مَالًا) mentioned in the previous ayah

steps.m: to make or produce something (especially children)

steps.n: Changed the root into a plural noun meaning 'sons' or 'children'

steps.m: sons/children (as object of gave/granted from previous verses)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) at the end to show this word is receiving the action from previous verses about what Allah granted

steps.m: and sons/children (as object of gave/granted from previous verses)
steps.n: Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous word
Changed from 'to witness' into 'witnesses' by making it plural - like how we say 'witness' becomes 'witnesses' in English
Added ًا at the end to show these witnesses are the object (thing being acted upon) in the sentence. This word is what 'we made' (جَعَلْنَا) them into.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Doubling middle letter makes the meaning more intense
Adding تُ changes subject to 'I' in past tense

steps.m: and I prepared thoroughly (for لَهُۥ تَمْهِيدًا)
steps.n: Added و for 'and' at start, with shadda on final ت for emphasis. This word connects to لَهُۥ تَمْهِيدًا (for him, a preparation) in the ayah.
This is the basic preposition ل that shows direction or purpose
Added fatha to help with pronunciation when connecting to next part
Added هُ which means 'him' to show who the action is for
Added the small و mark (ۥ) to show proper pronunciation. This word connects to تَمْهِيدًا (preparation) in the ayah, showing who the preparation is for.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes verb into a noun meaning 'the act of doing something' by adding تَ at start and ي after second root letter
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being prepared in the phrase 'and I prepared for him a complete preparation'
This is a sequential conjunction particle that connects events in time order

Added damma (ُ) on first letter and shadda with fatha (َّ) on last letter to show this word strongly connects what happened before with what happens next. In this ayah, it connects to someone's desire (يَطْمَعُ) for more after something else already happened.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he wants greedily (before أَنْ أَزِيدَ - that I increase)
steps.n: Added يَ at the start to show it's happening now, changed middle letter to have sukun (ْ), and added ُ at the end to show this is the main verb. This word comes before أَنْ أَزِيدَ (that I increase) showing what he wants.
This is a connecting word that shows what someone hopes or expects to happen. Here it connects 'he desires' with 'to increase'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the first vowel to kasra to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it
Added أ at start to make it causative passive - 'to be made to' do something
This word comes from the idea of stopping or preventing something. The shadda makes the 'l' sound stronger.

steps.m: No indeed! (strongly rejecting what came before)
steps.n: Adding the long alif at the end makes it stronger and more emphatic, like adding an exclamation mark in English
This is a special word that makes what comes after it more emphatic or certain, like adding an exclamation mark in English
Added هُۥ at the end to mean 'he'. This word connects to كَانَ (was) in the ayah, telling us who 'was'.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows the basic form before the weak letter change.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Changed the root into a noun for a single sign or miracle
Changed from singular to plural to show multiple signs
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose or direction, and kasra at end because another word will possess it
Added نَا at the end to show these are 'our' signs. The hamza and alif were written differently but mean the same thing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into an adjective that describes someone who is very stubborn by nature
Adds tanween alif to show this word is describing the subject of كَانَ (he was) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding hamza makes the verb causative - now means making someone else do the action

steps.m: he was made to carry a burden
steps.n: Changed to passive voice - someone is receiving the action instead of doing it

steps.m: I will make (someone) carry a burden
steps.n: Added س (sa) at start to mean 'I will' + changed to first person 'I' form

steps.m: I will make him carry a burden (صَعُودًا)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who receives the action (him). This connects to صَعُودًا (a difficult ascent) which explains what burden he will be made to carry.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: steep climb (something very hard to climb)
steps.n: Changed the middle vowel to damma and added و to show something that is very difficult to climb

steps.m: a steep climb (object of سَأُرْهِقُهُ - I will force him)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is what the action is being done to. In this ayah, Allah is saying He will force someone to climb something very difficult.
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain, like adding 'truly' or 'definitely' in English

steps.m: indeed he (refers to the person in فَكَّرَ - he thought)
steps.n: The هُ suffix means 'he' and shows who we're talking about. In this ayah, it refers to the same person who does the thinking (فَكَّرَ) in the next part.
The small ۥ mark is just a pronunciation guide, it doesn't change the meaning
This is the basic meaning of thinking or contemplating something
Connected the root letters with fathas to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: he thought very deeply (followed by وَقَدَّرَ - and he determined)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes the meaning more intense, showing careful and thorough thinking. This action is paired with determining/planning in the ayah.
The basic concept of measuring or determining something

steps.m: he measured precisely (after فَكَّرَ - he thought)
steps.n: The doubled د shows this was done with great precision and deliberation. This action follows after he thought deeply about what he would say.

steps.m: and he measured precisely (after فَكَّرَ - he thought)
steps.n: The وَ at the start means 'and', showing this careful measuring happened after he thought deeply
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show that the action happened to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: and so he was killed (in response to كَيْفَ قَدَّرَ - how he determined)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a response or result of what was mentioned before
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state/condition of something
The vowel marks help show this is a question word about manner/way
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more precise

steps.m: then (connects to قُتِلَ - he was destroyed)
steps.n: This is a sequential particle that shows one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects to what comes after it (قُتِلَ - he was destroyed), showing something happened and then he was destroyed.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it. In this ayah, it's used as a curse meaning 'may he be destroyed!'

steps.m: how (asking about the manner of قَدَّرَ)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens. In this ayah, it's asking about how someone determined or measured something.
Adding these vowel marks helps us pronounce the word correctly as 'kayf'
Basic meaning of measuring or determining something
Adding vowels to create basic past tense verb
Added shadda (double letter) on the middle letter to show intensity. This word is being cursed in the ayah through قُتِلَ (may he be destroyed).
This is the basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else

Added damma (ُ), fatha (َ), and shadda (ّ) to complete the word. The shadda makes the م sound stronger to emphasize the sequence. This word connects to نَظَرَ (he looked) showing he looked after something else happened.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb. This word comes after ثُمَّ (then) to show this looking happened after something else.

steps.m: then (connects to عَبَسَ وَبَسَرَ - he frowned and scowled)
steps.n: This word connects two actions in sequence, showing that frowning and scowling happened after something else. The ُ (damma) and َ (fatha) with ّ (shadda) make the word stronger.

steps.m: to make a stern or disapproving face
he frowned (followed by وَبَسَرَ - and he scowled)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This action is paired with the next word وَبَسَرَ (and he scowled) to show two related negative facial expressions.
Added 'wa' at the beginning to mean 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word comes after عَبَسَ (he frowned) in the ayah, showing two similar actions happening together.

steps.m: and he frowned angrily (after عَبَسَ/he frowned)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action with the previous word عَبَسَ (he frowned), showing these actions happened together.
This is a common connecting word that shows one thing happens after another

steps.m: then/next (followed by أَدْبَرَ - he turned away)
steps.n: Added ُ (damma) on ث and ّ (shadda) with َ (fatha) on م to show emphasis. This word connects to the next word أَدْبَرَ (he turned away) to show this action happened after something else.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he turned away (followed by وَٱسْتَكْبَرَ - and was arrogant)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning and sukun on first root letter makes the action more deliberate or intensive. This verb is paired with وَٱسْتَكْبَرَ showing two related negative actions.
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show someone trying to make themselves seem greater than they are

steps.m: and he acted arrogantly (after أَدْبَرَ/he turned away)
steps.n: Added و (and) at the beginning to connect it with the previous action of turning away
Added basic past tense vowels to show who did the action and when
Changed weak letter و to ا which is common in hollow verbs

steps.m: and then he said (introducing إِنْ هٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ يُؤْثَرُ - 'this is nothing but sorcery passed down')
steps.n: Added فَ to show this statement follows from and responds to something that happened before
This is a negative particle that makes a statement negative. Here it's used with إِلَّا (except/only) to say 'this is nothing but magic'
This is the basic attention-drawing part that starts many demonstrative pronouns
Added ذا which makes it point to something nearby. The alif above ه (ٰ) helps extend the sound.
Added fatha on ذ and extended alif at end for emphasis. This word is the subject of سِحْرٌ (magic) in the phrase 'this is nothing but magic'.
This is a basic exception particle that shows something is being excluded from what came before it. In this ayah, it's excluding what comes after it (magic) from what was mentioned before.

steps.m: except/only (excluding سِحْرٌ from what came before)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) and final alif (ا) complete the particle. In this ayah, it's used to say that what Pharaoh saw was nothing but/only magic, excluding all other possibilities.

steps.m: magic (that is being claimed about what was said)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun by adding the vowels ِ and ْ. This word is being used to describe what 'this' (هٰذَآ) is being called.
Added ٌ to show this word is the subject of what's being said
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it is being passed down (describing السِّحْرُ/magic)
steps.n: Changed to passive present tense to show this magic is being passed down from previous generations. This relates to the context where Pharaoh is claiming Moses's miracles are just inherited magic.

steps.m: if (introduces the condition 'this is nothing but human speech')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces the statement 'هٰذَآ إِلَّا قَوْلُ ٱلْبَشَرِ' (this is nothing but human speech). It suggests doubt or skepticism about what is being said.
Basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
Added small alif (ٰ) above ه to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added fatha on ذ to show clear pronunciation
Changed final alif to extended alif (آ) for emphasis in recitation
This is the base form of the exceptive particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. In this ayah, it's excluding 'human speech' from what was mentioned before.

steps.m: except (connecting to قَوْلُ ٱلْبَشَرِ - human speech)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to connect it smoothly with what follows. In this ayah, it connects to 'قَوْلُ ٱلْبَشَرِ' (human speech) to say 'this is nothing except human speech'
Changes from verb 'to say' into the noun 'a saying'
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence 'This is nothing but...'
Connected letters with fatha marks to create the noun form referring to humans
Adding the definite article makes the noun specific/definite
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is connected to something before it in the sentence

steps.m: to burn or roast with fire

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added أُ at start to show 'I' am doing the action, and changed vowels to make it causative (making something happen)
Added هِ to show who is receiving the action of burning

steps.m: I will cause him to burn
steps.n: Added سَ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future

steps.m: Saqar (a special name for a very hot level of Hell)
steps.n: This is a special name in the Quran for one of the levels of Hell. It comes from a word that means intense heat. In this ayah, Allah is warning that He will throw someone into Saqar.

steps.m: Saqar (object of سَأُصْلِيهِ - I will burn him in)
steps.n: The word Saqar here is receiving the action of the verb before it (سَأُصْلِيهِ) which means 'I will burn him in'. So Saqar is where someone will be burned.
This is a question word used to ask about things or seek information
Added و at the beginning to connect this question with what came before it
The alif is lengthened to add emphasis to the question and help with the flow of recitation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added 'أ' at the start to make it mean 'to make someone know' instead of just 'to know'
Added a small alif mark on top of ى to show it's pronounced with an 'aa' sound at the end
Added the pronoun كَ (you) as the object - the person being made to know
This is a question word used to ask 'what is it?' about something unknown. Here it's asking about سَقَرُ (Saqar).
This is the basic form of the word that became a name for Hell
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it a feminine noun
Changed the final vowel to damma to make it a proper noun name for Hell
This is a negative particle that makes verbs or statements negative
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added alif at beginning to make the verb causative - showing someone making something happen

steps.m: it/she is made to remain (negated by لَا)
steps.n: Changed to present passive form by adding تُ prefix and changing vowels. This word is negated by لَا before it, meaning 'it does not let remain'
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it's working with two verbs: تُبْقِى (it leaves) and تَذَرُ (it spares) to say it doesn't do either action.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
ت at start shows it's either 'she' or 'you' doing the action now, not in the past

steps.m: to scorch, burn, or make visible with heat

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
The doubled و and added ا show that this is someone/something that does this action intensively or repeatedly
Added ة to make it feminine since it describes نَارٌ (fire) which is feminine, and added tanwin (ٌ) to show it's indefinite
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a noun meaning mankind or humanity
Added لِلْ (combination of لِ meaning 'for' and الْ meaning 'the') at the beginning
The ل merged with another ل before it, shown by the shadda mark
Added a small diagonal line (kasra) under the last letter to show it connects with 'for' (لِ) before it
This is a common preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. It's one of the basic words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.

Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show intensity of the action. The و changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added مُ at the beginning to show who does the action (like adding -er in English). The ى changes to ي because of the next change coming.

Makes the word definite

The word is preceded by وَلَمْ which negates it and changes it to past tense. The final ن and و are dropped because of this negation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he fed' to present tense 'feed' by changing middle vowel from 'a' to 'i'
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. This word is connected to المِسْكِينَ (the needy) who are being fed.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added مِ at start and changed inside vowels to make a noun meaning 'someone who lacks settlement/resources' - opposite of the original verb meaning
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: the poor person (object of نُطْعِمُ - we feed)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is the object being fed in the phrase 'we feed the poor person'
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding fathas (َ) to make 'he was'
Changed to show 'they' (for females) instead of 'he' by adding a doubled 'n' sound at the end
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: and we were (connected to نَخُوضُ - we used to dive/plunge)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with the next verb نَخُوضُ (we used to dive/plunge)

steps.m: to dive into or engage deeply in (especially conversation)

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he dove into'
Changed from past tense (he dove) to present tense (dive) by changing the vowels from 'a' sounds to 'u' sounds

steps.m: we dive into (with مَعَ ٱلْخَآئِضِينَ - with those who engage in vain talk)
steps.n: Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. This word connects to مَعَ ٱلْخَآئِضِينَ showing who they were diving into conversation with.

steps.m: with (connects to ٱلْخَآئِضِينَ - those who indulged)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows doing something together with others. In this ayah, it's used to show that the speakers used to indulge (in falsehood) together with others who were doing the same thing.
The root word changed to show 'someone who does the action' by adding an alif after خ and an i-sound before ض. The و changed to آ because it came after a fatha sound.
Added ين at the end to show this refers to many people who do this action

steps.m: the ones who dive into (conversations/matters) (with نَخُوضُ - we dive into)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning to point to specific people. This word is connected to نَخُوضُ (we dive into) in the verse through the word مَعَ (with), showing these are the people they used to dive into discussions with.
Added fathas to make the past tense form. The middle weak letter و changes to ا between fathas.

steps.m: we were (subject of نُكَذِّبُ - we used to deny)
steps.n: Changed vowel to damma and added نَّا to show 'we' did this in the past. The ا from كَانَ drops because of this change.

steps.m: and we were (subject of نُكَذِّبُ - we used to deny)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing the action strongly or repeatedly
Changed to present tense with 'we' as the subject. This word connects to بِيَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ (Day of Judgment) which is what is being denied.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make it a proper word meaning 'day'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by الدِّينِ (the Judgment)
Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how or when something happened

steps.m: to follow a way or system

steps.n: Added kasra after first letter and long ي to create a noun meaning 'system that is followed'
Added ال to make it specific and shadda on د because it comes after ال

steps.m: of the religion/judgment (connected to يَوْمِ - Day)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word comes after يَوْمِ (Day) in يَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ (Day of Judgment)
Basic particle form showing endpoint or limit
Adding the shadda emphasizes the meaning

steps.m: until (shows end point of أَتَىٰنَا - came to us)
steps.n: Adding the alif maqsurah (ى) with an overextension mark (ٰ) shows this is the complete form of the word. This particle connects to the verb أَتَىٰنَا (came to us) to show when something ended.

steps.m: until (shows end point of أَتَىٰنَا - came to us)
steps.n: The small alif (ٓ) is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning
Changed from basic root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding fathas. The ى shows it's a special verb that ends with a weak letter.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above to show the long 'aa' sound clearly

steps.m: he came to us (subject of حَتَّىٰٓ until)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Forms a noun indicating complete or absolute certainty
Added ال to make it definite and ُ to show it's the subject
This is the basic interrogative particle that asks about something. In this ayah, it's asking about what benefit there could be.

steps.m: then what/so what (connects to تَنفَعُهُمْ - benefits them)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this question to what came before. It shows this question follows from a previous statement. In this ayah, it connects to تَنفَعُهُمْ (benefits them) to ask what benefit there could be.
Becomes a noun meaning the act of benefiting
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added damma at end to show it's in present tense normal form
Added هُمْ at the end to show who receives the benefit (them)

steps.m: to help someone by speaking for them
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root

steps.m: he helped by speaking for someone
steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: act of helping by speaking for someone
steps.n: Added alif after second letter to make it a noun meaning the action itself

steps.m: one instance of helping by speaking for someone
steps.n: Added ة to show one specific instance of the action

steps.m: he helped by speaking for someone
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: one who helps by speaking for others
steps.n: Adding alif after first root letter creates active participle form. This word is in the genitive case because it comes after شفاعة in a possession relationship.

steps.m: people who help by speaking for others
steps.n: Adding ين creates masculine plural form. This word is in the genitive case because it comes after شفاعة in a possession relationship, where شفاعة is the possessor.

steps.m: the people who help by speaking for others (negated by تَنفَعُهُمْ)
steps.n: Added ٱل (the) at the beginning and َ at the end. The word شَّ gets a shadda because it comes after ٱل. This word is being negated by تَنفَعُهُمْ (will not benefit them) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation. 'Ma' is an interrogative particle used to ask questions about non-human things, like 'what' in English.

steps.m: Then what (connects to مُعْرِضِينَ - turning away)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from something before. In this ayah, it connects to how they are 'turning away' (مُعْرِضِينَ).

steps.m: for/to them (who are مُعْرِضِينَ - turning away)

This is a basic preposition that shows distance or turning away from something. It's one of the core prepositions in Arabic that can't be broken down further.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the next word ٱلتَّذْكِرَةِ (the reminder). In this ayah, it shows they are turning away from the reminder.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'something that reminds'

steps.m: the reminder (object of عَنِ - away from)

Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action

steps.m: those who turn away (describes هُمْ/they in فَمَا لَهُمْ)

steps.n: This is the basic root meaning

This is the basic root meaning

steps.m: wildly running away (describing female donkeys from حُمُرٌ)

Added ت with sukun at the end to show this is about a feminine subject (referring to wild donkeys mentioned earlier)

Changed from basic root letters to a noun by adding vowels - shows the actual force/power rather than the action
Added و and ة to create a noun for something characterized by forceful power - in this case, a lion

steps.m: No! Rather... (contradicts what came before and introduces يُرِيدُ)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces the statement يُرِيدُ (he wants) to correct or contradict what was mentioned before. It shows a shift in topic.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas

Adding dhamma (ُ) to show this word is the subject
Changed from the root letters to make 'person' by adding vowels. This is who is doing the wanting in this ayah.
Added ٱ at start and changed first vowel to sukun to make the word definite
Added ٍ at the end to show this is describing an undefined person. This connects to كُلُّ (every) which comes before it in the ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added shadda for pronunciation because it comes after a word ending with a sukun

Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added shadda to middle letter to show intensive meaning
Added مُ at start to show this is happening to something rather than someone doing it
Added ة to make it feminine since it describes صُحُفًا (pages), and ً to match the case of the word it describes
This is the base form of the word that means to stop or prevent

steps.m: No indeed! (strongly rejecting what came before بَل لَّا يَخَافُونَ)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end makes this into an emphatic particle that strongly rejects what was said before. In this ayah, it's rejecting something and then بَل (rather) introduces what is actually true.

steps.n: This is a particle that strongly corrects what came before. Here it emphasizes the 'No!' (كَلَّا) and introduces the real situation - that they don't fear the Hereafter.
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'
The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the negation, making it stronger. This happens because it follows كَلَّا in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now, and ُ at the end to show it's a normal statement
Added ون to show many people are doing the action, with َ at the end to complete the word

steps.m: the Hereafter (object of يَخَافُونَ - they fear)

steps.n: Adding the long alif at the end makes this word express strong emphasis, like saying 'absolutely not!' or 'by no means!'. This word strongly rejects something before introducing the next statement that 'indeed it is a reminder'.
This is an emphatic particle that strengthens the meaning of what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that something is definitely a reminder (تَذْكِرَةٌ).

Added هُۥ (meaning 'it') to show what is being emphasized. This forms the subject of the sentence, while تَذْكِرَةٌ (reminder) is what's being said about it, creating the meaning 'Indeed, it is a reminder.'

Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'

Added fathas (َ) after each letter to make it past tense. This shows the action was completed.
Added هُ at the end to show what was remembered. This word is what was 'willed' in the phrase 'whoever willed'.
Basic particle that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's negating the verb يَذْكُرُونَ (they remember/mention)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural masculine ending ونَ to show multiple people doing the action

The long alif (آ) at the end adds emphasis and connects it smoothly to what follows. Here it introduces the exception to 'they remember' (يَذْكُرُونَ), showing that their remembering only happens with Allah's will.
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence together. Here it connects 'they do not remember' with 'Allah wills', showing that the second part depends on the first.
Added vowels and the weak letter ا replaced ي to make the past tense form

Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense. The long alif (آ) is used instead of regular alif (ا) for emphasis. This verb comes after إِلَّآ أَن (except that) which affects its meaning in context.

steps.m: one who is worshipped, a deity

This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it connects to أَهْلُ (people/family) to show 'He is the One worthy of being feared and the One worthy of forgiving'

steps.m: worthy ones (subject of this part of the ayah)

steps.m: to belong, to deserve, to be worthy

steps.n: Forms the basic noun meaning 'people/family' - those who belong to something
Added conjunction وَ meaning 'and'

steps.m: and [the] people (subject of this part of the ayah)
